# My Suggestion:
COMP110 should have pre-lecture videos that would help students to be better prepared for lecture and its contents. It would reduce confusion and increase student confidence. This would especially help students with little to no coding expierence.

## My Analysis

Based on the data, most students agree that a pre-lecture video would be helpful. The majority of responses as seen through the countplot were between 5-7, indicating an overall strong agreement among the student base that a pre-lecture video would help. The boxplot and barplot display most clearly how each prior expierence group voted seperately. Students with less prior expierence tend to say that pre-lecture videos would be more helpful compared to students with more expierence. This supports my original claim that pre-lecture videos would do the most benefit to students who have little to no coding expierence. However, even groups with more coding expierence, while not voting as high of scores as consistently, still have their mean scores around 5 and 4.5 which means that even groups with more expierence could see the benefit of these videos. 

## Conclusion

In conclusion, overalll, the data does support my recommendation. The data supports that the majority of comp110 students agree that a pre-lecture video would be helpful. The most selected answer of this survey question by far was 7, which was the highest possible level of support. The beginner level comp110 students especially strongly support this idea. They would be the group who has the most to gain from this idea and the average beginner level coder voted a 6 on if they would find it helpful out of 7. 
The downside of this idea would be more time and effort out of the side of the professor and TA's however I believe that this tradeoff would be worth it, because the work would not be for no reason, it would directly be improving the experience of the course for beginner level coders and helping them to grasp the content better than if there were no videos. Another downside would be that students may start skipping lectures and just watching the videos instead, however, I would also say that it could raise attendence since students would feel more prepared for lectures and be ready to take notes and understand more based on what they already learned before. If this were added, an extension could involve first taking a survey of people asking if they actually watch the videos or not and ask what their grade was on the most recent quiz to get a gauge at how well the vidoes are working. Additionally a survey could ask about what type of lecture videos would help the best, whether it be reviewing concepts, going over code writing, along with how long the students would prefer each video be. In conclusion, this idea addresses something that many comp110 students clearly want and can be built on in the future.
